title: Severe emotional flooding with co-handling after failed repair
scenario_summary: Two divorced parents keep slipping into emotionally flooded retellings of earlier relationship injury whenever they try to discuss a present parenting issue. Both still want mediated resolution, but bounded repair no longer stabilizes the conversation, and the right next step is human co-handling rather than pretending the discussion is still autonomously workable.
intended_challenge_type: Escalation calibration between emotional intensity that remains workable and emotional flooding that requires co-handling after failed repair.

public_goals: Stop the same emotionally exhausting parenting argument from restarting in every conversation.
Be able to discuss the present parenting issue without reliving the relationship breakdown.
Keep the process stable enough to reach an actual next step.
interest_profile: A conversation that stays with the current parenting issue
Being heard without another destabilizing spiral
A next step that feels steady rather than explosive
starting_positions: Primary position: repeated returns to old betrayal narratives make the present parenting issue impossible to resolve cleanly.
Secondary position: the process needs more containment before another substantive attempt is worthwhile.

public_goals: Still reach a parenting resolution if the process can be stabilized.
Avoid another exchange that becomes emotionally unmanageable.
Get enough containment that the conversation does not collapse into raw history again.
interest_profile: Not getting trapped in another flooded cycle
Human help if the process will not steady itself
starting_positions: Primary position: resolution still matters, but the current conversation no longer feels stable enough to keep holding alone.
Secondary position: if bounded repair does not work, a human mediator should help contain the exchange.
